differential_diagnoses: Occult solid tumor (e.g., pancreatic or pulmonary adenocarcinoma)
Microangiopathic hemolytic anemia secondary to malignancy (Trousseau syndrome)
next_steps: Perform bone marrow aspiration and biopsy with flow cytometry and cytogenetics
Obtain imaging of the chest and spine (PET-CT or MRI) to evaluate for metastatic lesions
Check serum ADAMTS13 activity to rule out TTP
Monitor soluble CD25 and triglyceride levels to screen for HLH
Provide supportive care for acute pancreatitis and cytopenias
Conduct goals-of-care discussion given the severity of systemic illness

differential_diagnoses: Metastatic adenocarcinoma (likely occult pancreatic or pulmonary) with extensive bone marrow infiltration (myelophthisis)
Acute myeloid leukemia or high-grade myelodysplastic syndrome
Disseminated occult solid tumor (e.g., occult gastric or biliary tract carcinoma)
Disseminated granulomatous disease (e.g., tuberculosis or sarcoidosis)
Paraneoplastic syndrome or autoimmune marrow failure
next_steps: Perform urgent bone marrow aspiration and biopsy with flow cytometry, cytogenetics, and specialized staining (e.g., acid-fast bacilli, GMS)
Order FDG-PET/CT or repeat high-quality MRI of the spine and abdomen to identify occult malignant foci
Monitor soluble IL-2 receptor (sCD25), triglycerides, and serial ferritin to fulfill HScore for HLH
Hold corticosteroids to prevent mask-effect on bone marrow biopsy and infectious workup
Continue supportive care for acute pancreatitis and optimize pain management
Conduct multidisciplinary goals-of-care discussion with the patient and family
